blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But there's no good way, is there? Did you come up with any plan to save the research club?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No. But I think that's a separate issue. Honestly, today… it must have been a really sad day for Ms. Siena. I don't know the details, but I can tell.
It was something everyone felt. She had bid farewell to someone she had cherished since childhood.
The fact that it ended with just a drink showed how strong Siena was.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But isn't it strange to get so sentimental? What's the point of living? Without using our heads, we're just corpses. The more we're like this, the more we need to stay calm. There's no immediate way to save the research club, so why push ourselves?
Iruki's words were also persuasive. If emotions clouded judgment, the future ahead would be nothing but hardship.
Neid's heart wavered slightly.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm. Honestly, Ms. Siena is a tough opponent. She has no weaknesses. Why else would we have gone this far? It's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.
Iruki nodded in agreement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's sharp-tempered, meticulous in planning, and thorough in managing students. Especially after what we heard before—how she dated Shirone to prevent an overflow and all that. How can we do anything against such a flawless opponent?
'These guys…!'
Siena, who had been listening with her eyes closed, felt a surge of anger. Her mood was gloomy, and her body was exhausted, but she couldn't just let it slide.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But now we know.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why she chose the path of a teacher, why she was so obsessed with overflow. Ms. Siena didn't want us to walk the same path she did.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Well, that's true, but…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter what made her become a teacher, the important thing is that she genuinely cares for us. But what about us? We're trying to attack her by exploiting her painful past. The seniors in the research club wouldn't want to see their juniors fall this far.
This time, even Iruki couldn't refute.
Magicians always pursued efficiency, but focusing too much on it could make them overlook what truly mattered.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: ...You always hit the nail on the head at times like this.
Neid, having made his decision, spoke lightly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Alright. Let's keep today's incident a secret among us. What do you say, Iruki?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: If it's not connected to the research club, I have no complaints. Honestly, I think Ms. Siena is the best when it comes to education.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, we're the villains here. Teachers like Ms. Siena, who care so meticulously for their students, are rare. And considering what we've done, she's been really lenient with us.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I'll let it slide this time. As for the research club, the three of us can put our heads together and come up with something, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, is that the plan? Even if we face disciplinary action tomorrow, what happened today stays buried. We'll never speak of it!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Deal! Sold!
Free from the torture of hope, they felt relieved.
Shirone and his friends walked with lighter steps than before.
None of them noticed that Siena, who was being carried on Shirone's back, had opened her eyes at some point.
The main gate of the magic school came into view.
Exhausted, Shirone stopped when he spotted a guard holding a torch.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What do we do? If we go back like this, rumors might spread. Plus, we left without permission.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No choice. Let's use magic from here. I'll levitate Ms. Siena, so you support her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait. Let's put her down first. Iruki, help me.
As he bent his knees, the weight on his back suddenly disappeared.
Startled, Shirone turned around to see Siena standing upright.
The drunkenness was gone, and her eyes were as clear as starlight.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: T-teacher?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher, are you really okay?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Of course. Did you think I was really drunk?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then why did you…?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You ate an expensive meal, so you should pay for it. Did you think you wouldn't have to suffer at all? Anyway, follow me. If you want to get back quietly.
Shirone and his friends followed Siena as if possessed.
The guard, recognizing her, greeted her with sleepy eyes.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Ah, good evening, Ms. Siena.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. Good work.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: But… did you go out with these students?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. I took them for fieldwork.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Ah, I see. Welcome back. Have a peaceful night.
Although students were forbidden from leaving at night, no one would doubt the word of Siena, who was highly respected in the school.
Nervous, Shirone and his friends quickly passed through the gate, worried the guard might call them back.
Once they safely reached the central park, Siena turned and glared at them.
Eek!
Only then did Shirone and his friends realize it—they had returned to Alpheas Magic School.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I'll let today's incident slide.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll let today's incident slide.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I'll give you another month starting today. That's the best negotiation card I can offer.
They understood what she meant. Resetting the grace period was her way of telling them to fight seriously.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Find a way to convince the school within a month. Whether it's holding a presentation or summoning a ghost, that's up to you. If you truly take pride in the research club, fight the school head-on and win what you want. That's my philosophy of education and how I show my love for my students.
Shirone and his friends were moved.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes! You can count on us! We'll definitely surprise the school!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Right! Struggling isn't our style! This time, we'll think it through properly and give it our all!
Siena smiled bitterly as she watched her students regain their confidence.
It was true that anxiety came first when their enthusiasm flared, but part of her also looked forward to what amazing things they might achieve.
'Haha. Do your best, you troublesome students.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Invisible (1)
Shirone, who had visited the research club later than usual, raised his hand weakly upon seeing Neid and Iruki lying on the sofa.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Did you sleep well last night?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know. I'm not even sure if I slept. What about you?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Same. What do we do now? We've been discussing all night, but no good ideas came up.
Shirone sat on the sofa and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We have to decide today. Let's come up with a plan.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: So, how? Supernatural parapsychology deals with invisible phenomena. But humans don't believe in what they can't see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We have to decide today. Let's come up with a plan.
The same conversation from last night repeated.
Too tired to even speak, they stared blankly at one spot.
It felt like their minds had gone blank.
After some time, Shirone's eyes regained focus.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What if we change our perspective?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: How?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, our dilemma is this: we have no way to show what's invisible.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Yeah. That's the big problem.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, what I mean is, if it's invisible, we don't have to show it. We've been clinging to the impossible, right? Maybe we can approach it from the angle of not showing it at all.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: If it can't be verified, then it doesn't need to be verified.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Exactly. The reason I joined the research club wasn't because you showed me something. So, wouldn't others be the same?
A faint smile appeared on Iruki's lips.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Why didn't we think of that? Right, verification wasn't necessary from the start. We didn't need to show what we didn't know. Rather, the opposite…

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Make them believe in the invisible?
Neid was convinced this was the answer he'd been seeking.
He immediately got up, hugged Shirone, and rolled around.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shirone! Come here! You saved our research club! You can even have my first kiss!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gross! Get off!
While Shirone and Neid tussled on the sofa, Iruki was lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So, what's the best way? Shirone's words are the solution, but we still need to give the presentation some form. We need tangible results.
Neid returned to his seat.
Shirone also smoothed his disheveled hair.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's think. What should we show?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: How about something auditory? It's easy to design.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's efficient, but not effective. We also have to consider whether the school will accept it.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Then let's go for something visual. If possible, auditory too. We'll pour everything we have into it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then what should we visualize? The supernatural? The paranormal?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The supernatural isn't mainstream. The paranormal seems better.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ghosts might be better. They're impactful.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But wouldn't that be childish?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I also think ghosts are better. The key is to strongly penetrate people's minds.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm. No matter how I think about it, it feels childish.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The first thing to consider is the effect. The rest is secondary. There's no need to give up.
